title: Reality Check Labs
description: Build immunity to manipulation and weak claims early. Boost classroom culture of 'show me the mechanism'.
why_it_exists: In an AI-shaped world, the ability to open a claim like a mechanic opens an engine is the most essential defense.

activities.title: Feed Hacker: Headline Autopsy
Signal vs Noise: The Two-Class Mystery
Model the Mess: The Data Plan Simulator
Odds Engine Arena: Expected Value, Not Hype
Evidence Ladder: Claim Strength Ranking
Research Hygiene: Detect P-Hacking & Tiny Samples
Truth & Reliability Ledger: Reputation is a Score

body:
# Reality Check Labs: The Defense Suite

This collection is designed to be the "immune system" of the Curiosity OS. It starts with simple headline autopsies and moves into the deeper logic of statistics and evidence.

## Learning Objectives
- Detect axis truncation and denominator blindness.
- Distinguish between a 'signal' and 'noise' in a distribution.
- Rank claims by evidence strength instead of loudness.
- Understand the 'Reliability Ratio' in reputation.

title: Build the Study Engine
description: Directly improve marks and confidence without 'content' tutoring. High-intensity memory and attention.
why_it_exists: Curiosity OS is a support to schooling. This collection turns the learning science backend into specific, performance-boosting study habits.

activities.title: Working Memory Budget: Build a Brain-Friendly Worksheet
Attention Lock Sprint: Single-Tasking Under Noise
Retrieval Showdown: Beat the Illusion of Competence
Interleaving Shuffle Lab: Spot the Hidden Differences
Practice Debug: Error Log Patterns (Beyond 'Silly Mistakes')
Growth Loops: The 1% Compounding Simulator

body:
# Build the Study Engine: Memory, Attention, Habits

This collection targets the most common failures in learning: rereading, multitasking, and ignoring error patterns.

## Learning Objectives
- Master **Retrieval Practice** as the primary study tool.
- Understand and reduce **Cognitive Load** in study materials.
- Implement a 15-minute **Attention Lock** sprint.
- Categorize mistakes into a **DEBUG Log** instead of a 'silly' bucket.
- Experience the power of **Compounding Habits** vs. Burst study.
筋

id: cos2_c03
slug: decision-gym
title: "Decision Gym"
description: "Teens face trade-offs constantly. Teach regret-aware planning, trees, and probability."
why_it_exists: "Decision making is the most expensive thing a mind does. A decision gym trains the 'muscles' of trade-off calculation before the pressure is high."
included_activity_ids: ["cos2_a05", "cos2_a13", "cos2_a15", "cos2_a18", "cos2_a29"]
featured_activity_id: "cos2_a15"
ordering_logic: "flexible"
---

# Decision Gym: The Strategic Thinking Suite

This collection moves students from "story-based" choosing to "evidence-based" deciding.

## Learning Objectives
- Map a choice as a **Decision Tree** with branches and costs.
- Attach **Confidence %** to declarations and update with Bayes logic.
- Understand the **Expected Value (EV)** of a bet vs its flashy story.
- Identify **Second-Order Effects** of a single policy change.
- Spot **Goodhart's Law** when chasing a single number.
筋
<!-- slide -->
---
id: cos2_c04
slug: truth-disagreement-studio
title: "Truth & Disagreement Studio"
description: "Preserve truth-seeking culture while avoiding toxic debates. Strengthen writing-as-thinking."
why_it_exists: "Disagreement is a signal of missing information, not an invitation to an argument. Building a truth-seeking studio preserves the classroom's curiosity."
included_activity_ids: ["cos2_a09", "cos2_a17", "cos2_a20", "cos2_a21", "cos2_a22", "cos2_a25"]
featured_activity_id: "cos2_a21"
ordering_logic: "flexible"
---

# Truth & Disagreement Studio: Synthesis & Steelman

This collection is about mapping the disagreement territory and finding the 'Deep Truth'.

## Learning Objectives
- **Steelman** the opposing view before critique.
- Draw an **Argument Map** of an ambiguous debate.
- Rank claims on the **Evidence Ladder** (consensus vs anecdote).
- Build a **Model Stack** of complex historical or scientific forces.
- Update a verdict publicly in **Bias Court** as new facts arrive.
筋
<!-- slide -->
---
id: cos2_c05
slug: people-pressure-defense
title: "People & Pressure Defense"
description: "Relate is explicitly defensive and maturity-building. High school survival tools."
why_it_exists: "Social pressure is the primary reason why teens abandon their own goals. Defense is as essential as cooperation."
included_activity_ids: ["cos2_a24", "cos2_a26", "cos2_a27"]
featured_activity_id: "cos2_a26"
ordering_logic: "flexible"
---

# People & Pressure Defense: The Social Shield

This collection provides tactical scripts and cues for staying safe and staying 'you' in a group.

## Learning Objectives
- Calibrate **Presence** using the Calibration Radar.
- Deploy a 5-word **Escape Pod** script under peer pressure.
- Build a **BATNA** (Best Alternative) for a social trap.
- Use **Boundary Scripts** to say NO without escalating.
筋
<!-- slide -->
---
id: cos2_c06
slug: trust-teamwork-upgrades
title: "Trust & Teamwork Upgrades"
description: "Improve group outcomes and classroom functioning. Reliability and cooperation."
why_it_exists: "Group work is notoriously difficult. These upgrades provide the 'logic' of cooperation and the 'mechanics' of repair."
included_activity_ids: ["cos2_a25", "cos2_a33", "cos2_a36", "cos2_a32", "cos2_a28"]
featured_activity_id: "cos2_a36"
ordering_logic: "intentional"
---

# Trust & Teamwork Upgrades: Logic of Cooperation

This collection is about building the 'Alliance Arena' in the classroom.

## Learning Objectives
- Rewrite a friction message to lower **Storm Probability**.
- Communicate under load using **Clear-Confirm** loops.
- Use **Tit-for-Tat** and Fairness protocols in games.
- Accuracy-match meaning using **Paraphrase, Clarify, Confirm** loops.
筋
<!-- slide -->
---
id: cos2_c07
slug: sandbox-make-tinker
title: "Sandbox: Make & Tinker"
description: "Turn concepts into competence. Build confidence through action and systems sight."
why_it_exists: "Without action, curiosity drifts into a cerebral trap. Tinkering builds the causal vision needed for real capability."
included_activity_ids: ["cos2_a32", "cos2_a29", "cos2_a18", "cos2_a04"]
featured_activity_id: "cos2_a32"
ordering_logic: "flexible"
---

# Sandbox: Make & Tinker (Systems Build)

This collection turns objects and scrappy materials into systems logic.

## Learning Objectives
- Build a **Scrap-to-System** machine with a causal build log.
- Experience **Outlier Noise** in a live data trial.
- See the **Domino Ripple** of a single pulley or lever choice.
- Understand **Incentive Designs** in physical objects.
筋
<!-- slide -->
---
id: cos2_c08
slug: sandbox-move-play-reset
title: "Sandbox: Move, Play, Reset"
description: "Regulation and social flexibility. Keeps the Operating System embodied."
why_it_exists: "Embodiment is the primary antidote to 'Transfer Collapse'. We move to learn, and we play to calibrate."
included_activity_ids: ["cos2_a30", "cos2_a31", "cos2_a34", "cos2_a35"]
featured_activity_id: "cos2_a34"
ordering_logic: "flexible"
---

# Sandbox: Move, Play, Reset (Embodied Regulation)

This collection is about calm, motion, and the timing of a group.

## Learning Objectives
- Rate physical **Risk** before moving into the 'Outlaw' zone.
- Navigate a **Spatial Hunt** using map representation vs reality.
- Run a structured **Zen Reset** after a stress peak.
- Practice **Social Timing** in the Chaos Carnival.
筋
